## `struct wpa_driver_ops`

- **File:** `src/drivers/driver.h`
- **Definition line:** 1633 (`struct wpa_driver_ops {`)
- **Closing brace:** line 3442 (`};`)
- **Function-pointer fields:** **142**

Confirmed via `grep`/`awk` (LSP `goToDefinition` on the struct tag resolved to the same location, `src/drivers/driver.h:1633:8`). I counted every field matching the `(*name)(...)` function-pointer pattern within the struct body (lines 1633–3442); no duplicates or comment false-positives were found.

Representative fields (first ~20 of the 142):
`get_bssid`, `get_ssid`, `set_key`, `init`, `deinit`, `set_param`, `set_countermeasures`, `deauthenticate`, `associate`, `add_pmkid`, `remove_pmkid`, `flush_pmkid`, `get_capa`, `poll`, `get_ifname`, `get_mac_addr`, `set_operstate`, `mlme_setprotection`, `get_hw_feature_data`, `send_mlme`, …

…continuing through driver-specific and later-added callbacks such as `scan2`, `authenticate`, `set_ap`, `hapd_init`, `sta_add`, `if_add`/`if_remove`, `send_action`, `remain_on_channel`, `tdls_oper`, `wnm_oper`, `signal_poll`, `vendor_cmd`, `sched_scan`, `switch_channel`, MACsec ops (`macsec_init`, `enable_protect_frames`, `create_receive_sa`, `create_transmit_sa`, etc.), mesh ops (`init_mesh`, `join_mesh`, `leave_mesh`), and ending with `do_acs`, `set_band`, `get_pref_freq_list`, `set_prob_oper_freq` (last one at line 1809 in the sed-offset listing, i.e. absolute line 3441 in the file).

No files were modified.
